flags.concern: Shot positioning/order: the plan says new shots can be populated as unpositioned and even suggests a Promise.all fallback for batch insert, but the repository's video-generation flow only treats positioned images as valid input. `runAddImageMutation()` maps `timelineFrame: null` to an unpositioned insert, and `useVideoGenerationModalController` filters to positioned images then blocks generation when none exist. On the timeline side, `selectedClipIds` is an insertion-ordered Set, so Step 7 also needs an explicit sort by clip `at`/track order before assigning frames or the created shot will not preserve timeline order.
flags.evidence: Revised plan to address all 3 flags: (1) Items are now POSITIONED via sequential `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration` calls — not unpositioned. Timeline clips are sorted by `(trackIndex, action.start)` before insertion. (2) Shot naming uses existing `generateShotName()` from `useShotCreation` — no new naming convention. (3) Cross-surface selection clarified as independent (mutually exclusive by design); no selection merging attempted. Also removed Phase 4 floating action bar per user instruction ("skip, just do context menu for now"). Added gate for non-media timeline clips (hide menu items entirely). Simplified hook architecture — no separate `useCreateShotFromSelection` or `useGenerateVideoFromSelection` hooks; instead extend existing `useShotCreation` with a `generationIds` option and a new creation path.

flags.concern: Shot naming: Step 2 invents a new default like 'Shot from N items', but the repository already centralizes auto-naming in `useShotCreation()` as `Shot ${count + 1}` and the user explicitly approved that convention. A parallel naming rule in a new hook will diverge from the rest of the app.

flags.concern: Cross-surface selection: the plan explicitly narrows 'across both' to 'whichever system has a selection', but the repository currently enforces mutual exclusion between gallery and timeline selection. If the product requirement still means combining selected gallery items and selected timeline clips into one shot or video action, this plan does not touch the selection model that prevents it.
